title: Slack Integration
summary: Slack integration using Socket Mode and the Bolt SDK, covering app manifest generation, required bot scopes, event subscriptions, Messages Tab, multi-workspace support, thread-command prefix, and per-channel prompt and skill bindings.
key_concepts: Socket Mode: no public URL required
App manifest generation via hermes gateway slack manifest
Required bot scopes and event subscriptions
Messages Tab for direct app DMs
Multi-workspace support via separate app installs
App-level token vs bot token distinction

env_vars.name: SLACK_BOT_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Bot OAuth token (xoxb-...) from app installation

env_vars.name: SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET
env_vars.description: Signing secret for HTTP mode webhook verification (not needed for Socket Mode)

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including Slack listener
Generate the Slack app manifest JSON for easy app creation
Reset current Slack session (use ! prefix for thread commands)

config_keys.description: App-level token for Socket Mode (overrides SLACK_APP_TOKEN)
Channel IDs the bot responds in
Map of channel_id to system prompt override
Map of channel_id to list of skill names to enable

code_examples.title: Generate Slack app manifest
code_examples.code:
hermes gateway slack manifest
# Outputs JSON to paste into api.slack.com/apps > Create App > From Manifest

code_examples.title: Per-channel prompts and skills
code_examples.code:
slack:
  per_channel_prompts:
    C0123456789: "You are a customer support agent for AcmeCorp."
  per_channel_skills:
    C0123456789:
      - crm_lookup
      - ticket_create

code_examples.title: Thread commands (in Slack thread)
code_examples.code:
!reset
# Resets the session for this thread
!help
# Shows available commands

gotchas: Socket Mode requires an app-level token (xapp-...) in addition to the bot token (xoxb-...); these are different token types
The app manifest includes all required scopes; manually created apps often miss scopes causing silent failures
Commands inside threads must use the ! prefix (configurable); regular slash commands are not supported in thread context
Messages Tab must be enabled in app settings for users to DM the app directly
Multi-workspace support requires separate Slack app installs per workspace; tokens are workspace-scoped
Slack rate limits: tier 1 (1/min), tier 2 (20/min), tier 3 (50/min) vary by API method
Socket Mode WebSocket connections may drop; hermes auto-reconnects but brief gaps can occur
full_text:
## Slack

Connect Hermes to Slack using Socket Mode (no public URL required).

### Setup

#### 1. Generate the app manifest

```bash
hermes gateway slack manifest
```

This prints a JSON manifest with all required scopes and event subscriptions pre-configured.

#### 2. Create the Slack app

1. Go to [api.slack.com/apps](https://api.slack.com/apps) and click **Create New App → From Manifest**.
2. Paste the generated manifest JSON.
3. Install the app to your workspace.

#### 3. Get your tokens

- **Bot token** (): Under **OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token**
- **App-level token** (): Under **Basic Information → App-Level Tokens** → generate with  scope

### Minimal config

```yaml
slack:
  bot_token: ""
  app_token: ""
  allowed_channels:
    - C0123456789
```

Start the gateway:

```bash
hermes gateway
```

### Messages Tab

Enable **Messages Tab** in your app's **App Home** settings to let users DM the app directly. DMs always bypass .

### Thread commands

In Slack threads, commands use the  prefix (not ):

```
!reset
!help
```

Configure the prefix:

```yaml
slack:
  thread_prefix: "!"
```

### Per-channel prompts

Override the system prompt for specific channels:

```yaml
slack:
  per_channel_prompts:
    C0123456789: "You are a customer support agent for AcmeCorp. Only answer questions about our products."
```

### Per-channel skill bindings

Enable only specific skills in certain channels:

```yaml
slack:
  per_channel_skills:
    C0123456789:
      - crm_lookup
      - ticket_create
```

### Multi-workspace

To support multiple Slack workspaces, create a separate Slack app install for each workspace and configure multiple Slack integrations in the gateway config:

```yaml
slack:
  workspaces:
    - bot_token: ""
      app_token: ""
    - bot_token: ""
      app_token: ""
```
